body: {"answer": "Enron used special purpose entities (SPEs) for a few key reasons:\n\n1. To move assets and debt off its balance sheet and improve its reported financial results. By moving assets and debt into SPEs that were not fully consolidated, Enron was able to hide losses and make its financial position appear stronger than it really was. 2. To structure controversial partnerships that allowed Enron to hide debt, mask investment losses, and inflate earnings. The SPEs were used to create complex financial arrangements involving derivatives, Enron stock, and promissory notes that obscured the true nature of Enron's financial situation. 3. To facilitate Enron's profitable derivatives trading business. The SPEs enabled Enron to act as an intermediary for trading energy derivatives and other commodity contracts through its Enron Online division, generating significant revenue that masked problems in its core energy business.", "sources": [{"text": "(ENRNQ) scandal. Enron used SPEs, some contro=\r lled by its own executives, to move assets and debt off its balance sheet a=\r nd thus improve its financial results; the now-bankrupt company later recon=\r solidated some of the SPEs into its balance sheet, which reduced its previo=\r usly reported earnings by $586 million.=20\r Companies can form non-consolidated SPEs apart from their balance sheets ev=\r en though outside investors might provide as little as 3% of the entities' =\r capitalization.
